source_book: NCERT Class 11 Physics Part I Ch.8 + HC Verma Ch.4

real_world_anchor.primary: A mango falls from a tree. Nobody pushed it. Nothing was tied to it. Earth reaches across empty space and pulls it down.
real_world_anchor.secondary: A magnet attracts a paper clip through a pane of glass. No contact — the field does the work.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: A compass needle swings toward north with no wire, no touch. Earth's magnetic field is guiding it.

constraints: w = m * g at all times
mass and weight are not the same physical quantity
field forces act across empty space — no contact or medium is required
every mass creates a gravitational field; every charge creates an electric field

STATE_1.title: The Mango Falls — Nobody Pushed It

scene_composition.text:
Nothing is touching it.
No string. No push.
Yet it keeps falling.

tts_sentences.text_en: A mango falls from a tree. Nobody pushed it. No rope was attached.
Watch the gap between positions — it keeps getting larger. That is acceleration from gravity.
Everyone knows the name — gravity. Today we uncover how gravity actually works.
Because gravity belongs to a special family of forces — forces that act across empty space.

STATE_2.title: Earth and Mango — 6,400 km Apart, Zero Contact

scene_composition.text:
No contact at all —
and yet Earth still pulls.

tts_sentences.text_en: The center of Earth sits six thousand four hundred kilometers below the surface. The mango sits on a branch above.
There is no string between them, no solid contact. Only air fills the gap.
And yet Earth pulls the mango. The force crosses empty space as if the gap were not there.
This is the puzzle of field forces — distance alone cannot switch them off. Tap continue.

STATE_3.title: The Gravitational Field — Invisible Force Carrier

scene_composition.label:
Any mass
here feels a pull

scene_composition.text: Earth creates a gravitational field that reaches every point around it.

tts_sentences.text_en: Earth builds a gravitational field around itself. The dashed arrows show that field pointing inward.
The field is everywhere — above, below, left, right. Every direction is covered.
Drop any mass into this field and it immediately feels a downward pull. No touching required.
The field is the messenger. Forces travel through fields, which is how nature delivers a push without direct contact.

STATE_4.title: Weight Is the Field Force on Your Body: w = mg

scene_composition.text:
The downward pull of the gravitational field on a body
is what we call its weight.

tts_sentences.text_en: Put a one kilogram mass into the gravitational field. It immediately feels a downward pull of nine point eight newtons.
That specific force has a name: weight. Its formula is w equals m times g.
Here m is the body's mass and g is the strength of Earth's gravitational field at its surface.
Weight is a force, measured in newtons. It is not the same thing as mass, which is measured in kilograms. Tap continue.

STATE_5.title: You Control It — Mass Slider, Weight Scales Linearly

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag the mass slider and watch what happens.
Increase the mass and the weight arrow grows. Decrease it and the arrow shrinks.
The relationship is linear — double the mass, double the weight. Always.
And this force is never switched off. As long as Earth exists, its field exists, and so does your weight.

annotations.text: Through the origin: zero mass means zero weight.

regeneration_variants.label: Charged Balloon on a Wall
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A rubbed balloon clings to a wall. The electrostatic field between them reaches across an air gap.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: A force acts without contact — the field carries it.
regeneration_variants.key_difference: Electric field E replaces gravitational field g. Force equals qE, not mg.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Balloon body with electric field lines fanning toward the wall. A visible air gap. No contact anywhere.

regeneration_variants.label: Magnet and Iron Filings
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A horseshoe magnet attracts iron filings spread on a sheet of paper. The magnetic field passes right through the paper.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: One body creates a field, another body feels the resulting force — no contact needed.
regeneration_variants.key_difference: Magnetic field B. The force depends on magnetic moment, not mass.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Magnet body, iron filings at various distances, visible magnetic field lines.

regeneration_variants.label: Weight on the Moon
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A two kilogram stone on the Moon. The Moon's g is 1.6 m/s^2, so the stone weighs 3.2 N — not 19.6 N.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: w = mg still applies. Only g changes when you change planets; mass does not.
regeneration_variants.key_difference: g is planet-specific. Mass is a property of the body. Weight is not.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Planet toggle (Earth / Moon). Weight arrow shrinks to one sixth on the Moon while the mass label stays the same.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Weight and mass are the same thing — both measured in kilograms.
trigger_phrases: weight and mass are the same

scene_composition.text: Student's belief: 'Weight = 60 kg'

Weight is a FORCE.
Forces are measured in newtons, not kilograms.

tts_sentences.text_en: You are saying your weight is sixty kilograms. This is one of the most common mistakes in physics.
Weight is a force. Forces are measured in newtons, not kilograms.
Sixty kilograms is your mass — how much matter your body contains.
Your weight is sixty times nine point eight, which equals five hundred eighty eight newtons. That is the downward pull of gravity on you.

EPIC_C_2.title: The Moon Test — Mass Stays, Weight Changes

scene_composition.text: Same mass. Different weight.

If weight equalled mass,
the Moon reading would still be 60.
It is not — because weight is a force,
not a property of the body.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same body, same mass — but placed on two different planets.
On Earth the weight is five hundred eighty eight newtons. On the Moon it is only ninety six newtons — six times smaller.
The mass did not change at all. Only g changed.
If weight equalled mass, the Moon reading would still say sixty. It does not, because weight and mass are not the same quantity.

left_scene.text:
How much matter a body contains.
Unit: kilogram (kg).
Same everywhere — Earth, Moon, deep space.
Scalar quantity.

right_scene.text:
The gravitational force on a body.
Unit: newton (N).
Changes from planet to planet.
Vector quantity (points downward).

scene_composition.text:
Exam rule of thumb:
If the unit is kg, you are talking about mass.
If the unit is N, you are talking about weight.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the distinction is clear. Mass is a property of the body — a measure of matter.
Weight is a gravitational force — it depends on both m and g.
This distinction matters in exam problems, especially when g changes between parts of a question.

epic_c_branches.misconception: A force can only act if the bodies are in physical contact.
trigger_phrases: how can force act without contact

EPIC_C_1.title: Your Belief: No Contact Means No Force

left_scene.text:
A hand pushes a cart.
Touching: YES.
Force: obvious.

right_scene.text:
Earth pulls a mango.
Touching: NO.
Force: still real.

scene_composition.text: Intuition says: 'No touch, no push.'

But nature disagrees.
Let us see how.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your intuition says a force cannot exist without touching. That instinct is natural.
Push a table and it moves. Contact is visible. Cause and effect are obvious.
But nature has two families of forces — contact forces and field forces.
Field forces — gravity, magnetism, electrostatics — work across empty space with no touching at all.

EPIC_C_2.title: Four Real Field Forces — All Without Contact

scene_composition.right_label: Magnetism (Magnet pulls iron through a gap)

left_scene.text:
Field: gravitational (g).
Acts on: any mass.
Range: infinite.

right_scene.text:
Field: magnetic (B).
Acts on: moving charges and magnets.
Range: infinite but weakens fast.

scene_composition.text: Electrostatic and nuclear field forces complete the set of four field families in nature.
Four fields. Zero contact. All real.

tts_sentences.text_en: Four fundamental forces in nature — gravity, magnetism, electrostatics, and the strong nuclear force — all act across space.
Each one works the same way: one body creates a field around itself.
Any other body placed in that field immediately feels a force.
The field is real and physical. It is nature's substitute for direct contact.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Gravity (or any field) needs air or some medium to travel through — it cannot work in a vacuum.
trigger_phrases: how does gravity travel through empty space

EPIC_C_1.title: Your Belief: Gravity Needs a Medium

scene_composition.text:
Apollo 15 moon experiment:
No air at all — yet feather
and hammer both fall together.
The field does not need a medium.

tts_sentences.text_en: You are wondering — if there is no air, how does gravity reach across?
In 1971, astronaut David Scott dropped a feather and a hammer on the Moon. No air at all.
Both hit the ground together. Gravity worked perfectly without a single molecule of air in between.
Fields are not sound waves. They do not need a medium. They exist in space itself.

scene_composition.text: VACUUM — zero molecules

Rule: a field is not carried BY anything.
The field itself is the physical fabric
that lets one mass pull another across
empty space.

tts_sentences.text_en: Think of the field as the medium itself. The field exists even in total vacuum.
A satellite in orbit is in near-perfect vacuum, yet Earth pulls it every second. That pull is exactly what keeps it in orbit.
Sound waves need air. Water waves need water. But gravitational and electromagnetic fields need nothing at all.
So the answer to 'does gravity work in vacuum' is: yes — it works best in vacuum, because nothing can block or absorb it.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Gravity is one-way — Earth pulls the mango, but the mango does not pull Earth.

EPIC_C_1.title: Your Belief: Only Big Things Pull

scene_composition.text: ? Mango -> Earth ?

Most students draw only ONE arrow.
They think the mango is too small to pull.
Newton's third law says otherwise.

tts_sentences.text_en: You draw one arrow — Earth pulling the mango. Most beginners stop there.
The idea that only big objects create gravity feels natural, but it is wrong.
Newton's third law says every force has an equal and opposite partner.
So if Earth pulls the mango with 9.8 newtons, the mango must pull Earth with 9.8 newtons too. Both arrows exist.

EPIC_C_2.title: Both Arrows Are Real — Why You Never Notice

scene_composition.text:
Why you never see Earth move:
Earth's mass is huge, so its acceleration
is so tiny that no instrument can detect it.
The force is there — the motion is hidden.

tts_sentences.text_en: The mango pulls Earth with exactly nine point eight newtons — the same force, pointing upward.
So why does Earth not visibly jump toward the mango? Because Earth's mass is six times ten to the twenty four kilograms.
The resulting acceleration is about ten to the negative twenty four meters per second squared. Utterly unmeasurable.
Remember — force is mutual. Motion depends on mass. Every mass pulls every other mass, always.

STATE_1.description: Identify the scenario: a body (mango) falls freely with no contact. State that a force is acting and name it as gravity.

STATE_2.description: Classify gravity as a field force (action at a distance). State that it crosses empty space with no medium required.

STATE_3.description: Define gravitational field g as the region around Earth where any mass experiences a force. State units: N/kg or m/s^2.

STATE_4.description: Write the formula w = mg. Define each symbol with units. State that weight is a force (newtons), not the same as mass (kilograms).

STATE_5.description: Show numerical example: substitute m = 1 kg and g = 9.8 m/s^2 to get w = 9.8 N. State that w is linear in m.

primitives.text: Step 1: Observation — a mango falls from a tree with no contact.
Step 2: A force must be acting on the mango (otherwise no acceleration).
Step 3: Name this force gravity — directed toward Earth's center.
+1 for identifying the force
Step 4: Gravity acts without contact — it is a FIELD FORCE.
Step 5: Field forces cross empty space; no medium is needed.
Step 6: Other field forces: electric, magnetic, nuclear.
+1 for field force classification
Step 7: Define gravitational field g = region where any mass feels a pull.
Step 8: At Earth's surface, g = 9.8 N/kg = 9.8 m/s^2, directed toward the centre.
Step 9: Field = force per unit mass: g = F / m.
+1 for defining g
Step 10: Rearrange g = F / m to get F = mg.
Step 11: This force is called WEIGHT, symbol w.
Step 12: w = mg   [w in newtons, m in kg, g in m/s^2].
+1 for w = mg with units
Step 13: Substitute m = 1 kg, g = 9.8 m/s^2:
Step 15: Weight is linear in mass: doubling m doubles w.
+1 for numerical answer

shortcuts.use_when: Any problem that mentions a body's mass near Earth's surface; g defaults to 9.8 m/s^2 unless stated.
Problem switches to Moon, Mars, Jupiter, or any different g. Mass stays the same; recompute weight with the new g.

shortcuts.rule: Gravitational field at height h above Earth
shortcuts.use_when: Altitude is non-negligible compared to Earth's radius R (e.g., satellites).
Quick sanity check on any exam answer involving gravity.

shortcuts.formula: a = g (downward) when gravity is the only force
shortcuts.use_when: No air resistance, no contact — object is in free fall. Works for feathers in vacuum too.
Two or more masses create overlapping fields; add them as vectors at the test-mass location.

edge_cases.title: Apparent weight in an accelerating lift
edge_cases.trap: Students write w = mg regardless of the lift's acceleration.
edge_cases.resolution: Apparent weight (reading on a spring balance) is N = m(g +/- a). Use + when the lift accelerates upward, - when downward. True weight mg is unchanged.

edge_cases.title: Astronauts floating in the ISS
edge_cases.trap: Students say there is no gravity in space.
edge_cases.resolution: Gravity is very much present (g ~ 8.7 m/s^2 at ISS altitude). Astronauts float because the station and astronaut are in continuous free fall around Earth — apparent weight is zero, true weight is not.

edge_cases.title: Mass of a body in deep space
edge_cases.trap: Students assume mass becomes zero when weight is zero.
edge_cases.resolution: Mass is a property of the body and never changes with location. Only weight changes. Inertia (F = ma) still works in deep space — push the body and it still resists.

edge_cases.title: g at the equator vs poles
edge_cases.trap: Students treat g = 9.8 m/s^2 as universal.
edge_cases.resolution: g is larger at poles (~9.83) than equator (~9.78) because of Earth's rotation and oblateness. Board problems usually use g = 9.8; competitive problems may specify otherwise.

edge_cases.title: Charged mass in combined gravitational and electric field
edge_cases.trap: Students add mg and qE as if they were the same kind of force.
edge_cases.resolution: Both are field forces and both act independently. Total force = mg (down) + qE (along E). Sum as vectors; they do NOT cancel unless geometrically arranged to.
